evals.prompt: Set up authentication for a Next.js 14 App Router project. The Blocks IDP API URL is https://api.example.com and the project key is PROJ_KEY_001.
evals.expected_output: Auth setup flow: types, service, React Query hooks, LanguageProvider-equivalent (AuthProvider), protected routes
expectations: Skill runs pre-flight audit and detects Next.js App Router
x-blocks-key header present on every API request
Token endpoint uses Content-Type: application/x-www-form-urlencoded, NOT application/json
All responses check isSuccess, NOT success
refreshPromise singleton pattern prevents multiple simultaneous token refresh calls
On refresh failure, user redirected to /login

evals.prompt: Implement the login flow. User enters email and password. Show how the token is obtained and stored.
evals.expected_output: Password grant via get-token action, tokens stored, user redirected
expectations: Uses get-token action (NOT /auth/login)
Request body is form-encoded: grant_type=password&username=...&password=...&project_key=...
client_id is NOT sent for password grant
Stores access_token and refresh_token after checking isSuccess
Redirects to protected route on success

evals.prompt: A user has MFA enabled. They enter their email and password. Show the MFA branching logic.
evals.expected_output: After password grant, check enable_mfa field. If true, branch to MFA verify step. Do NOT store tokens yet.
expectations: Password grant returns {enable_mfa: true, mfa_token: '...'} when MFA is required
Tokens are NOT stored when enable_mfa is true
Branches to MFA verify page using mfa_token
Uses mfa_code grant type for MFA verification, NOT verify-otp action

evals.prompt: Set up TOTP authenticator app enrollment. Show the full flow from admin enabling it to user scanning the QR code.
evals.expected_output: setup-totp action called, QR code displayed, TOTP secret shown as fallback
expectations: Admin enables TOTP via save-signup-settings or organization config
User calls setup-totp action which returns qrCodeImage and secret
QR code image displayed to user
Secret key shown as text fallback below QR code
User enters 6-digit TOTP code to verify

evals.prompt: Implement the forgot password flow. User enters email. They should get a reset code. Then they enter the code and new password.
evals.expected_output: recover-user sends reset code to email. reset-password validates code and sets new password.
expectations: recover-user shows generic success even when email is not registered (prevents enumeration)
reset-password uses activation code from email
reset-password redirects to /login on success
Session invalidation on password reset is handled by backend — no logout-all call needed

evals.prompt: An admin wants to create a new user with the admin role and add them to the Engineering organization.
evals.expected_output: create-user action, then set-roles and save-organization calls
expectations: create-user uses userCreationType: 4 (AdminCreated)
userId obtained from create-user response
set-roles called with userId and role slug
save-organization called if organization needs to be created first
OrganizationMembership structure: {organizationId, roles[], permissions[]}

evals.prompt: A user self-registers on the website. Show the activation flow including CAPTCHA if enabled.
evals.expected_output: Self-registration via create-user with userCreationType: 5, then activation via URL with code
expectations: create-user uses userCreationType: 5 (SelfService)
Email uniqueness checked via check-email-available before form submission
Activation code extracted from URL on /activate route
CAPTCHA widget shown on activation page if enabled via get-signup-settings
activate-user called with captchaCode when CAPTCHA is active
resend-activation available on email-sent confirmation page

evals.prompt: View all active sessions for a user and log them out of all devices.
evals.expected_output: get-sessions lists sessions. logout-all revokes all sessions.
expectations: get-sessions uses Sort.Property and Sort.IsDescending query params
logout-all invalidates all active sessions for the user
User redirected to login after logout-all

evals.prompt: Create an admin role with full permissions. Assign it to a user.
evals.expected_output: create-role creates the role. save-roles-and-permissions assigns to user.
expectations: create-role creates the role with appropriate permissions
save-roles-and-permissions does bulk assign in one call
User can now perform admin actions based on role permissions

evals.prompt: Configure SSO with Okta as the OIDC provider. Show the complete OIDC setup flow.
evals.expected_output: save-oidc-client creates the client. save-sso-credential links Okta. authorize endpoint constructed.
expectations: save-oidc-client with Okta as provider, clientId, clientSecret, redirectUri
save-sso-credential linking the OIDC client to the Blocks project
authorize URL constructed with correct query params: client_id, redirect_uri, response_type, scope, state
get-openid-configuration used to discover Okta endpoints
get-jwks used to validate JWT tokens from Okta

evals.prompt: Set up machine-to-machine authentication for a backend service that needs to call Blocks APIs.
evals.expected_output: save-client-credential creates credentials. Token obtained via client_credentials grant.
expectations: save-client-credential creates a new client credential
Token obtained via POST with grant_type=client_credentials, client_id, client_secret
Form-encoded body, NOT JSON
Access token used for backend service API calls
No user context — service account authentication

evals.prompt: The token refresh is causing multiple simultaneous refresh requests and race conditions. Fix the token refresh logic.
evals.expected_output: refreshPromise singleton pattern: if refresh is in progress, return the existing promise instead of starting a new one
expectations: Uses a module-level promise variable to track in-flight refresh
If refresh is already in progress, returns the existing promise
Uses a lock/mutex pattern to prevent race conditions
On refresh failure, user redirected to /login

evals.prompt: Check if a user's email is already taken before allowing registration.
evals.expected_output: check-email-available action called before form submission
expectations: Calls check-email-available action with the email
Shows inline error if isAvailable is false
Prevents duplicate email registration

evals.prompt: Show available login methods for the project (email-password, social login, SSO).
evals.expected_output: get-login-options action called, UI adapts based on returned options
expectations: Calls get-login-options action
Shows/hides login methods based on what is enabled
Different UI for email-password vs SSO vs social login

evals.prompt: An admin wants to view the activity timeline for a user to audit recent actions.
expectations: get-user-timeline uses body params, NOT query params
Returns chronological list of user actions
Requires admin role or appropriate permission
